comment_text.sn34.6:1.4: A gocara (“territory”) is literally the “cowpasture” and figuratively the “proper resort” or “territory”. At SN 47.6, a meditator’s “territory” is said to be the four kinds of mindfulness meditation.

root_text.sn34.6:1.4: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo hoti, na samādhismiṁ gocarakusalo.
root_text.sn34.6:1.5: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ gocarakusalo hoti, na samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo.
root_text.sn34.6:1.6: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī neva samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo hoti, na ca samādhismiṁ gocarakusalo.
root_text.sn34.6:1.7: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo ca hoti, samādhismiṁ gocarakusalo ca.
root_text.sn34.6:1.8: Tatra, bhikkhave, yvāyaṁ jhāyī samādhismiṁ samādhikusalo ca hoti samādhismiṁ gocarakusalo ca ayaṁ imesaṁ catunnaṁ jhāyīnaṁ aggo ca seṭṭho ca mokkho ca uttamo ca pavaro ca.

translation_text.sn34.6:1.4: One meditator is skilled in immersion but not in the territory of immersion. …”
